[{"motor_ref":"7bf56531","motor_type":"MultipleChoice","motor_json":"{\"problemText\":\"Up to an altitude of about $10\\\\;\\\\mathrm{km}\\\\,,$ air temperature usually decreases with height above the Earth's surface. The sun warms the Earth's surface, which in turn warms up the nearby air. When a \\\"temperature inversion\\\" forms, there is warmer air above colder air instead. \\n\\n[b]Which of these ray diagrams shows refraction due to a temperature inversion?[\\/b]\\n\\n[asy]\\n\\/\\/https:\\/\\/artofproblemsolving.com\\/texer\\/vldzfekp\\n\\/\\/ ============================================================\\n\\/\\/ Sound rays in a temperature gradient \\u2014 parabolic version\\n\\/\\/\\n\\/\\/ Each ray follows a parabolic path:\\n\\/\\/ x(t) = cos(theta) * t\\n\\/\\/ y(t) = sin(theta) * t + 0.5 * k * t^2\\n\\/\\/\\n\\/\\/ k > 0 \\u2192 rays curve upward (temperature inversion)\\n\\/\\/ k = 0 \\u2192 straight rays\\n\\/\\/ k < 0 \\u2192 rays curve downward (normal daytime gradient)\\n\\/\\/ ============================================================\\n\\nsize(14cm, 10cm);\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- adjustable parameter ----------\\nreal k = -.1; \\/\\/ try: 0.3 (up), 0 (straight), -0.3 (down)\\n\\/\\/ ------------------------------------------\\n\\n\\/\\/ Source location\\npair src = (0, 0);\\n\\n\\/\\/ Number of rays\\nint nRays = 24;\\n\\n\\/\\/ Number of steps used to draw each ray (more = smoother curve)\\nint nSteps = 200;\\n\\n\\/\\/ Maximum t value \\u2014 controls how far rays extend\\nreal tMax = 6;\\n\\n\\/\\/ Ray color and style\\npen rayPen = rgb(145\\/255, 39\\/255, 37\\/255) + linewidth(0.6pt);\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- ground line ----------\\nreal groundY = -1;\\ndraw((-7, groundY) -- (7, groundY), black + linewidth(1.5pt));\\nlabel(\\\"Ground Level\\\", (0, groundY), S, fontsize(9pt));\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- draw each ray ----------\\nfor (int i = 0; i < nRays; ++i) {\\n\\n \\/\\/ Launch angle, evenly spaced around full circle\\n real theta = 360.0 * i \\/ nRays;\\n real thetaRad = theta * pi \\/ 180;\\n\\n real cosT = cos(thetaRad);\\n real sinT = sin(thetaRad);\\n\\n \\/\\/ Build the path by stepping t from 0 to tMax\\n \\/\\/ We stop early if the ray goes below ground\\n path rayPath;\\n bool started = false;\\n\\n for (int j = 0; j <= nSteps; ++j) {\\n real t = tMax * j \\/ nSteps;\\n\\n real x = src.x + cosT * t;\\n real y = src.y + sinT * t + 0.5 * k * t^2;\\n\\n \\/\\/ Stop drawing this ray if it hits the ground\\n if (y < groundY) break;\\n\\n pair pt = (x, y);\\n\\n if (!started) {\\n rayPath = pt;\\n started = true;\\n } else {\\n rayPath = rayPath -- pt;\\n }\\n }\\n\\n if (started) draw(rayPath, rayPen, Arrow);\\n}\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- source dot ----------\\nfill(circle(src, 0.09), black);\\n\\n[\\/asy]\\n\\n[asy]\\n\\/\\/https:\\/\\/artofproblemsolving.com\\/texer\\/vldzfekp\\n\\/\\/ ============================================================\\n\\/\\/ Sound rays in a temperature gradient \\u2014 parabolic version\\n\\/\\/\\n\\/\\/ Each ray follows a parabolic path:\\n\\/\\/ x(t) = cos(theta) * t\\n\\/\\/ y(t) = sin(theta) * t + 0.5 * k * t^2\\n\\/\\/\\n\\/\\/ k > 0 \\u2192 rays curve upward (temperature inversion)\\n\\/\\/ k = 0 \\u2192 straight rays\\n\\/\\/ k < 0 \\u2192 rays curve downward (normal daytime gradient)\\n\\/\\/ ============================================================\\n\\nsize(14cm, 10cm);\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- adjustable parameter ----------\\nreal k = 0; \\/\\/ try: 0.3 (up), 0 (straight), -0.3 (down)\\n\\/\\/ ------------------------------------------\\n\\n\\/\\/ Source location\\npair src = (0, 0);\\n\\n\\/\\/ Number of rays\\nint nRays = 24;\\n\\n\\/\\/ Number of steps used to draw each ray (more = smoother curve)\\nint nSteps = 200;\\n\\n\\/\\/ Maximum t value \\u2014 controls how far rays extend\\nreal tMax = 6;\\n\\n\\/\\/ Ray color and style\\npen rayPen = rgb(61\\/255, 92\\/255, 100\\/255) + linewidth(0.6pt);\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- ground line ----------\\nreal groundY = -1;\\ndraw((-7, groundY) -- (7, groundY), black + linewidth(1.5pt));\\nlabel(\\\"Ground Level\\\", (0, groundY), S, fontsize(9pt));\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- draw each ray ----------\\nfor (int i = 0; i < nRays; ++i) {\\n\\n \\/\\/ Launch angle, evenly spaced around full circle\\n real theta = 360.0 * i \\/ nRays;\\n real thetaRad = theta * pi \\/ 180;\\n\\n real cosT = cos(thetaRad);\\n real sinT = sin(thetaRad);\\n\\n \\/\\/ Build the path by stepping t from 0 to tMax\\n \\/\\/ We stop early if the ray goes below ground\\n path rayPath;\\n bool started = false;\\n\\n for (int j = 0; j <= nSteps; ++j) {\\n real t = tMax * j \\/ nSteps;\\n\\n real x = src.x + cosT * t;\\n real y = src.y + sinT * t + 0.5 * k * t^2;\\n\\n \\/\\/ Stop drawing this ray if it hits the ground\\n if (y < groundY) break;\\n\\n pair pt = (x, y);\\n\\n if (!started) {\\n rayPath = pt;\\n started = true;\\n } else {\\n rayPath = rayPath -- pt;\\n }\\n }\\n\\n if (started) draw(rayPath, rayPen, Arrow);\\n}\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- source dot ----------\\nfill(circle(src, 0.09), black);\\n\\n[\\/asy]\\n\\n[asy]\\n\\/\\/https:\\/\\/artofproblemsolving.com\\/texer\\/vldzfekp\\n\\/\\/ ============================================================\\n\\/\\/ Sound rays in a temperature gradient \\u2014 parabolic version\\n\\/\\/\\n\\/\\/ Each ray follows a parabolic path:\\n\\/\\/ x(t) = cos(theta) * t\\n\\/\\/ y(t) = sin(theta) * t + 0.5 * k * t^2\\n\\/\\/\\n\\/\\/ k > 0 \\u2192 rays curve upward (temperature inversion)\\n\\/\\/ k = 0 \\u2192 straight rays\\n\\/\\/ k < 0 \\u2192 rays curve downward (normal daytime gradient)\\n\\/\\/ ============================================================\\n\\nsize(14cm, 10cm);\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- adjustable parameter ----------\\nreal k = .1; \\/\\/ try: 0.3 (up), 0 (straight), -0.3 (down)\\n\\/\\/ ------------------------------------------\\n\\n\\/\\/ Source location\\npair src = (0, 0);\\n\\n\\/\\/ Number of rays\\nint nRays = 24;\\n\\n\\/\\/ Number of steps used to draw each ray (more = smoother curve)\\nint nSteps = 200;\\n\\n\\/\\/ Maximum t value \\u2014 controls how far rays extend\\nreal tMax = 6;\\n\\n\\/\\/ Ray color and style\\npen rayPen = rgb(65\\/255, 98\\/255, 22\\/255) + linewidth(0.6pt);\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- ground line ----------\\nreal groundY = -1;\\ndraw((-7, groundY) -- (7, groundY), black + linewidth(1.5pt));\\nlabel(\\\"Ground Level\\\", (0, groundY), S, fontsize(9pt));\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- draw each ray ----------\\nfor (int i = 0; i < nRays; ++i) {\\n\\n \\/\\/ Launch angle, evenly spaced around full circle\\n real theta = 360.0 * i \\/ nRays;\\n real thetaRad = theta * pi \\/ 180;\\n\\n real cosT = cos(thetaRad);\\n real sinT = sin(thetaRad);\\n\\n \\/\\/ Build the path by stepping t from 0 to tMax\\n \\/\\/ We stop early if the ray goes below ground\\n path rayPath;\\n bool started = false;\\n\\n for (int j = 0; j <= nSteps; ++j) {\\n real t = tMax * j \\/ nSteps;\\n\\n real x = src.x + cosT * t;\\n real y = src.y + sinT * t + 0.5 * k * t^2;\\n\\n \\/\\/ Stop drawing this ray if it hits the ground\\n if (y < groundY) break;\\n\\n pair pt = (x, y);\\n\\n if (!started) {\\n rayPath = pt;\\n started = true;\\n } else {\\n rayPath = rayPath -- pt;\\n }\\n }\\n\\n if (started) draw(rayPath, rayPen, Arrow);\\n}\\n\\n\\/\\/ ---------- source dot ----------\\nfill(circle(src, 0.09), black);\\n\\n[\\/asy]\\n\",\"barChartText\":null,\"type\":\"MultipleChoice\",\"parseFormat\":\"selectedIndices\",\"outputDisplayFormat\":\"selectedValues\",\"barChartFormat\":null,\"options\":{\"items\":[\"a)\",\"b)\",\"c)\"],\"multiSelect\":true},\"problem_text_fmt\":\"Up to an altitude of about air temperature usually decreases with height above the Earth's surface. The sun warms the Earth's surface, which in turn warms up the nearby air. When a "temperature inversion" forms, there is warmer air above colder air instead.
\\n
\\nWhich of these ray diagrams shows refraction due to a temperature inversion?<\\/b>
\\n
\\n
\\n
\\n
\\n
\\n
\\n\"}"}]
solution_text:
The temperature inversion corresponds with option $ oxed{	ext{a)}}\,.$

Upward moving rays bend away from the normal, indicating they move into an area with faster wave speed. That's warmer air, so option a) represents warm air above cool air.

Option b) represents uniform air, and option c) represents the usual temperature gradient (cool air above warm air).

Use the simulation to explore how the temperature gradient bends the rays.

[center]<iframe src="https://aops-curriculum.github.io/539_Middle_School_Physics_1/Sound/sound_refraction_sim.html" width="600" height="500" scrolling="no" allowfullscreen></iframe>[/center]
